response.justification: Candidate has 16+ years of solid software engineering experience but the C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015. The recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/full-stack work, with no evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443 SDL, MQTT, message brokers, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850). The profile is a strong full-stack/web engineer, not a senior C++ platform developer for industrial automation.
strengths: 16+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Demonstrated craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development, public talks and workshops
Some networked, low-latency, micro-services experience (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, millions of req/s)
concerns: C++/STL experience is minimal (~6 months in 2015) and over a decade stale
No evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443 SDL, MQTT, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP3)
Recent stack is JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang focused — not aligned with high-performance C++ platform role

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / IEC 62443 / MQTT background
Strong general engineering tenure and craftsmanship practices
